steps.title: Spot the contrasting ideas
steps.content: The passage lists Hammurabi's military conquests and city-building achievements, then shifts to what he is “mainly remembered for” — a code of laws. The transition must signal this unexpected turn from multiple grand accomplishments to one specific legacy.

steps.title: Apply the contrast signal
steps.content:
”However” captures this contrast precisely: despite his military and architectural achievements, he is mainly remembered for the Code instead. “Therefore” creates false causation, “likewise” wrongly implies similarity, and “for instance” misrepresents the Code as an example of his other accomplishments.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
